EQP-1: Provides a function f defined on a finite domain and a value v to be searched.
EQP-2: The domain of f is assumed to be well-defined and finite.
FUN-1: The system searches for an index i such that f(i) = v.
FUN-2: In the abstract model m0, the final condition requires that i belongs to dom(f) and f(i) = v.
FUN-3: In the refinement m1, the system introduces a Boolean variable searching that indicates whether the search is ongoing. When searching is true, the procedure explores indices starting from n−1 downward.
FUN-4: Initialization sets i := n−1 to establish loop invariants.
FUN-5: If f(i) ≠ v, then the algorithm decrements i and continues.
FUN-6: A Boolean variable found represents whether the target has been located. When found becomes true, it must hold that f(i) = v and searching becomes false.
SAF-1: During the search, if searching is true, the condition v ∈ f[0..i] must always hold, ensuring that the target value remains within the considered subdomain.
SAF-2: At all times, the condition i ∈ dom(f) must hold. This ensures the algorithm never discards potential indices incorrectly.
SAF-3: When found is true, the property f(i) = v implies that the abstract requirement i ∈ dom(f) ∧ f(i) = v is satisfied.
DEG-1: If v is not present in the function image f[dom(f)], then found never becomes true and the search cannot succeed.
DEG-2: If the function f is undefined on some indices, the invariant i ∈ dom(f) may be violated.
